purpose: Instructions for creative job detail collection and API-ready export via pipeline/enrichFromCsv.ts

gpt_enrichment_pipeline.description: A standalone post-processing pipeline that reads the already-enriched results_enriched_api.csv and uses OpenAI GPT-4.1 nano to validate and fix every job row. Works purely from existing CSV data — no web fetching, no Playwright.

options.--maxJobs <n>: Process at most N jobs (useful for testing)
options.--apiKey <key>: OpenAI API key (or set OPENAI_API_KEY env var)

usage.test_first: Run with --maxJobs 5 to verify output quality before processing the full dataset

gpt_enrichment_pipeline.drop_safety_net: Code-level override prevents dropping rows where the title looks like a real job title or the URL matches a specific job posting pattern. Overridden rows get a fallback description: 'For more details, click apply'.
gpt_enrichment_pipeline.csv_safety: Description HTML is forced to single-line (no newlines) both via prompt instructions and post-processing to prevent Excel CSV cell overflow.
gpt_enrichment_pipeline.input_preprocessing:
Input description is HTML-entity-decoded before sending to GPT (e.g. <p> ->

) to prevent double-escaping when the source CSV already contains escaped HTML.
gpt_enrichment_pipeline.output_postprocessing: Emojis are stripped from GPT output via regex (covers Unicode emoji blocks U+1F000-1FFFF, misc symbols U+2600-27BF, variation selectors). GPT is also instructed to remove emojis in the prompt.
gpt_enrichment_pipeline.bold_rule: <strong> is used ONLY for section heading labels (About the Role, Responsibilities, Requirements, etc.). Regular body text, list items, and sub-labels must NOT be bolded.

ollama_enrichment_pipeline.description: A standalone post-processing pipeline that reads the already-enriched results_enriched_api.csv and uses a local Ollama LLM (qwen3.5) to validate and fix every job row. Unlike enrichFromCsv.ts which fetches live pages, this script works purely from the existing CSV data — no web fetching, no Playwright.
ollama_enrichment_pipeline.purpose: Quality pass over all jobs to ensure field correctness, description cleanliness, and structured HTML formatting — for free and with unlimited calls via local Ollama.

options.--concurrency <n>: Parallel Ollama calls (default: 2, keep low for local model)
options.--maxJobs <n>: Process at most N jobs (useful for testing)

usage.test_first: Run with --maxJobs 5 to verify output quality before processing the full dataset

title.action: Fix generic/careers-page headings (e.g. 'Asana Careers'). Infer real title from description or URL if possible. Mark INVALID if truly unfixable.
title.drop_condition: If title is INVALID AND job_url also does not point to a real job

description.action:
Strip all noise (cookie banners, login prompts, EEO statements, salary sections, apply forms, generic company boilerplate). Keep only job-relevant content: responsibilities, qualifications, about-the-role, required skills. Format as clean structured HTML using

Title

for section headings (no heading tags),

for all body text,

    /
      /
    1. for lists, / for emphasis. Every text block must be wrapped in

      . Break single-paragraph blobs into logical sections.
description.output_format: Compact single-line HTML (no newlines within the HTML string)
description.heading_rule:
Do NOT use

/

/

heading tags. Use

Section Title

for section headings instead — renders at the right size and weight in the web app.

jobType.action: Validate against allowed values. Fix using mapping rules. Default to FULLTIME if unknown.

workType.action: Validate against allowed values. Infer from description/title/location if missing or incorrect. Clear if truly unknown.

location.action: Validate location makes sense. If city is 'Remote', clear all location fields and ensure workType=REMOTE. Fix obvious errors. Clear if nonsensical.

job_url.action: Read-only. Passed to AI as context only. Never modified.

ollama_enrichment_pipeline.drop_logic: Drop a row ONLY when BOTH title cannot be determined to be a real job title AND the jobLink URL does not point to a real job posting (e.g., it's a careers listing page, homepage, 404, or error page). If EITHER the title looks like a real job title OR the URL looks like a specific job posting URL (contains /job/, /careers/specific-role, /position/, job ID in path, etc.), the row MUST be kept. When keeping a row with a poor/empty description, set description to 'For more details, click apply'.
ollama_enrichment_pipeline.fallback_behavior: If AI returns invalid JSON or times out, the row is kept with basic heuristic validation (jobType/workType normalization) rather than dropped. If description cannot be meaningfully cleaned, it defaults to 'For more details, click apply' rather than being dropped.
ollama_enrichment_pipeline.passthrough_fields: All other CSV columns (keywords, skills, deadline, salary, company, hiringTeam, workEmail, etc.) are passed through unchanged — this pipeline only touches the 6 reviewed fields.

phases: Phase 1: Fetch pages (HTTP first, Playwright fallback for JS shells / ATS domains)
Phase 2: Heuristic extraction (JSON-LD + scored HTML description candidates + text patterns)
Phase 3: AI enrichment via Claude Haiku (for incomplete records: short descriptions, missing skills/salary)
Phase 4: Merge heuristic + AI results (prefer better quality description, merge arrays, keep structured fields)
Phase 6: Quality review (heuristic checks + AI review for ambiguous cases)
Phase 7: Output (JSON, CSV, report, pipeline instructions)

fields_to_extract.source: JSON-LD title -> h1 tag -> seed CSV title
fields_to_extract.notes: Filters out careers-page headings (e.g. 'Asana Careers'), strips junk suffixes ('@ Lever', '| LinkedIn'). Flagged for AI fix if title looks generic.

fields_to_extract.source: Final URL after redirects (from Playwright or HTTP fetch)
fields_to_extract.notes: Maps to API jobLink. /apply path suffix stripped. Canonicalized for dedup (UTM params removed).

fields_to_extract.source: Scored HTML selectors (ATS-specific first) -> cleaned text fallback -> JSON-LD description -> meta description
fields_to_extract.notes: Store frontend-safe rich HTML only (not raw full-page wrappers). Candidate scoring favors responsibilities/qualifications structure and penalizes cookie/login/apply-form content. Apply-form sections (Submit application, Resume/CV, First Name, etc.) are trimmed.
Maps to { placeId, name, formattedAddress, latitude, longitude, city, state, country }. Country codes expanded (US -> United States). 'Remote' as city -> workType=REMOTE, location cleared.

Entity prefixes stripped (ADUS-Adobe Inc. -> Adobe Inc.). URL derivation: careers.X.com -> X, ATS paths (greenhouse.io/company -> company). Company email is backfilled from extracted workEmail when available.

fields_to_extract.source: Keyword inference from title + description + location
JSON-LD baseSalary -> description text pattern matching -> AI extraction
fields_to_extract.notes: Maps to { min, max, currency, period }. Text extraction matches $80,000-$120,000/year and 80k-120k patterns.

fields_to_extract.source: JSON-LD occupationalCategory/industry/qualifications + heuristic keyword terms + AI extraction
fields_to_extract.notes: Tags are represented in API as keywords. Lowercased and deduplicated.

fields_to_extract.source: JSON-LD skills + heuristic skill taxonomy/phrase parsing + AI extraction
fields_to_extract.notes: Normalized to lowercase/underscore canonical ids and deduplicated (e.g., figma, design_systems, ux_research).

fields_to_extract.source: mailto links + email regex over HTML/description
fields_to_extract.notes: Prefers non no-reply addresses. Also copied to company.email when company.email is missing.
Set true when workEmail exists, otherwise false.

description_html_rules.goal: Preserve meaningful rich-text structure while removing page chrome and oversized container markup
description_html_rules.wrapper_handling:
Unwrap/flatten article/div/main/section/span wrappers; convert simple div text groups to

; strip all attributes except safe anchor href
description_html_rules.anchor_policy: Keep links only for http(s)/mailto href. Remove javascript and empty links.
description_html_rules.minimum_content_rule:
If cleaned rich HTML has too little plain text (< 80 chars), fallback to escaped plain text in a

block.

csv_description_serialization.rule: description must remain a single CSV field; no delimiter breakage
csv_description_serialization.formatting: Normalize description to compact single-line HTML before CSV escaping
csv_description_serialization.important: Do not inject visual line breaks/newlines inside HTML solely for spreadsheet readability; rely on valid CSV quoting.

fetching_strategy.http_first: Plain HTTP fetch with 2 retries for static pages
fetching_strategy.js_shell_detection: If HTTP returns >2KB HTML but <500 chars visible text, detected as JS shell -> Playwright retry
fetching_strategy.playwright_domains: greenhouse.io, lever.co, smartrecruiters.com, myworkdayjobs.com, icims.com, ashbyhq.com, phenompeople.com, amazon.jobs, workable.com
fetching_strategy.playwright_subdomains: careers.*, career.*, jobs.*, job.*, apply.*, hire.* subdomains always use Playwright
fetching_strategy.playwright_config: networkidle wait + 2000ms extra for JS rendering

ai_enrichment_triggers.missing_salary: Neither JSON-LD nor text pattern found salary

heuristic_checks: Title: blocklist (cookie, login, 404), length bounds, junk suffix stripping, careers-page heading detection
Description: blocklist (cookies banner, login wall), placeholder detection, page chrome stripping, unformatted blob flagging
Company: generic name detection + URL-based fix, entity prefix stripping, joined-word flagging
WorkType: validation + contradiction detection (ONSITE vs 'fully remote' in text)
Location: junk removal, 'Remote' as city -> workType conversion
ai_review_triggers: Generic company names still remaining after heuristic fix
Unformatted blob descriptions (>500 chars, no formatting)

collecting_job_info.deduplication: title (lowercased) + canonicalized job URL as compound key
collecting_job_info.source_metadata: hiringTeam field set to uploader UID (default: system-scraper); allowEmailApplications enabled when workEmail/company.email exists

transformations.notes: Map to object: { name, website, logo, email }
Use unified object { placeId, name, formattedAddress, latitude, longitude, city, state, country }

example_output.title: Senior Product Designer
example_output.description:
Own end-to-end product design across web and mobile experiences.

  • Lead design for core product features
